title: Acid dissociation constant (Ka)
short: The equilibrium constant for a weak acid giving up its proton; a larger Ka means a stronger acid.
full:
A weak acid HA only partly ionizes in water: HA ⇌ H⁺ + A⁻. Its acid dissociation constant Ka = [H⁺][A⁻]/[HA] measures how far that ionization goes. A larger Ka (or smaller pKa = −log Ka) means a stronger acid that gives up its proton more readily.

For a weak acid of initial concentration c, the small-x approximation gives [H⁺] ≈ √(Ka·c) when ionization is slight, from which the pH follows. This is the standard route to the pH of a weak-acid solution such as acetic acid.
